p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Yellow pot; dense foliage cluster; turgid leaves; no visible wilting.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Stable throughout the 5-day sequence. No significant change in leaf orientation or color.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_reasoning: High resilience. The turgidity remains consistent with the previous report, indicating successful moisture retention.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Black pot; two primary leaves; one central sprout; soil surface shows white particulate matter.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: The central sprout has maintained its vertical posture since T-3. The white particulate matter (user-added) remains stable.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_reasoning: The plant is recovering from previous stress. The lack of further drooping suggests the soil moisture is now adequate.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Black pot; two leaves; white rabbit scale anchor (5cm); one leaf shows marginal necrosis.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The necrotic lesion on the lower leaf has remained static since the earliest image. The rabbit anchor is unchanged.
p3_pothos.visual_health_reasoning: Stable. The necrosis is not progressing, suggesting the VPD stress is currently managed.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Small sprout located near the rim of the p2/p4 shared pot; single leaf structure.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: The leaf has maintained its angle relative to the pot rim throughout the sequence.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_reasoning: Appears healthy and stable; no signs of chlorosis or wilting.

biome_observations.soil_surface: Consistent presence of white particulate matter across pots, confirming successful user-applied soil amendments.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clear of debris; no fungal growth or moisture leakage detected.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: The sequence shows a transition from a state of potential dehydration (T-4) to a stabilized state (Current). The most notable change is the cessation of progressive necrosis in p3 and the stabilization of p2's posture.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of 'Managed Equilibrium'. The previous stress indicators (drooping in p2, necrosis in p3) have plateaued, suggesting that the environmental adjustments and user interventions have been effective.
vision_report.anomalies: None. All visual changes are consistent with user care and natural growth cycles.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms that the garden has moved past the acute stress phase noted in the previous report. p1 remains the most robust, while p2 and p3 show clear signs of stabilization. The white particulate matter on the soil surface is confirmed as a successful user intervention. The biome is currently stable.
